key_insight: Any FEM-like multiscale basis with fixed locality and fixed coefficient access cannot achieve uniformly vanishing error on rough-coefficient elliptic problems; its worst-case energy error stays O(1) as H→0.
engineering_relevance: This gives a sharp negative design rule for scalable multiscale solvers: to get uniform accuracy, you must enlarge the stencil/patch size, increase local spectral dimension, or access more global coefficient information.
method: Defines scalability by bounded overlap, fixed support radius, and fixed coefficient-information radius.
Proves an order-one lower bound for deterministic constructions under these fixed local constraints.
Uses periodic coefficient counterexamples, corrector approximation lower bounds, and mesh-density arguments.
evidence_signal: The paper proves the result analytically using a fixed finite family of smooth periodic coefficients and smooth right-hand sides, rather than a numerical benchmark.
watch_item: The impossibility result targets deterministic, fixed-locality constructions, so practical methods may still work if they adapt patch size, basis richness, or coefficient visibility with H.
paper_title: An Order-One Lower Bound on the Error of Scalable Generalized Multiscale Finite Element Space Constructions
